title: Python Execution, Tools, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Python Style Formatting
summary: Build and diagnose a small command-line learning log across REPL, script, editor, and terminal workflows. Planned artifact: Add and verify the python style formatting increment in a reproducible command-line learning log. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply readable naming, formatting, comments, docstrings, and small commits as correctness-supporting tools.
Use an editor, terminal, working directory, and command history without losing file or environment context.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
